approach: Specify the correction procedure only; do not perform #282's sweep. The output is the procedure #282 consumes.
Verify the Clearance-routing claim against the real classifier rather than asserting it from the issue; route the rule on the removal, not on the edit class the diff reports.
State the re-derivation duty once in closed form as a process rule, decreeing no slack constant, and lint only the vacuous sum(max) < threshold case.
Freeze the rule as additive spec-producer scenarios; edit no frozen scenario.
blast: bar change across one reference spec node, one shipped governance skill, and the producer bar, plus additive scenarios in one frozen suite and one additive fail-closed lint with tests in the gate's own check-suite engine; no CLI, no schema, no corpus edit
why: auto-spec: the change is confined to governance prose, additive scenarios that self-clear, and one additive lint whose boundary is bound in both directions by tests. The corpus correction it governs stays with #282. The Clearance question on this CR's own suite is settled mechanically — additive, 7 added / 0 modified / 0 removed — and charged to the cold judge regardless.

Within the auto-spec leash. Two cold judge rounds; round 1 returned ALIGNED false on the builder lens for six leaked rubric nouns in four authored scenarios — a defect the producer's own mechanical form check catches every time and which I had not run over my own output. Fixed by rewording to the cut vocabulary the prose already uses, not argued. Round 2 returned ALIGNED true on all three lenses after independently re-deriving the routing correction with its own synthetic fixture, re-running every tool, and arguing the strongest case against the CR's central claim that its vacuity lint is not a banned slack constant; the distinction survived — sum(max) < threshold is unsatisfiability of the collapsing Then, independent of judge noise, whereas the banned constants are guesses at cSEM. sum(max) == threshold is deliberately left legal and unflagged. The routing rule is stated on the removal rather than on the edit class: measured against the real classifier, the in-scenario correction shape reports narrowing and only a separate added Scenario reports mixed, so the issue's own mixed-therefore-Clearance reasoning is unsound though its conclusion holds. No Conflict with the frozen :172 scenario — it governs pre-clearance raising, the new scenarios govern post-clearance application on a disjoint Given. The lint's guard is bound in both directions by ablation (neutering it fails the two detection tests) and by an over-fire mutant (relaxing strict < to <= fails the two zero-slack controls), and it fires on the real corpus rubric #282 will correct: operator.feature dimensions total 5 against threshold 7. pnpm verify green.

title: The dead-rubric lint scans every DocString on a graded scenario, not the one the collapsing Then carries
why: findDeadRubric is applied to each DocString attached to a @rubric scenario. A scenario carrying a second, unrelated DocString whose lines coincidentally match the max and threshold patterns would be reported against, or reported twice. The failure direction is over-reporting only — it can never mask a genuine dead rubric — and no scenario in the corpus carries a second DocString today, so it does not fire. Raised by the cold impl-judge as an architect observation and left out of scope because closing it means scoping the scan to the DocString the collapsing Then carries, which no frozen scenario specifies either way.

title: The corpus figures #282 is scoped against are stale and its scope is drawn one repo short
why: Measured while scoping this CR's procedure. The corpus holds 37 real rubrics counted by threshold lines rather than the filed ~19, of which cyberfleet holds 12; the ~13 conjunctive dimensions figure is accurate but one of them sits in the aced spec rather than cyberfleet, which the issue does not mention, and two more are conjunctive in substance while escaping the name pattern entirely. Naive deletion makes four rubrics unpassable and five more passable only at a flawless score, a case the issue's nothing-can-pass framing does not name. Belongs as a correction on the consuming issue rather than a new one.
